name: tju_spin_deps_delpartner
drl:
rule TJ1 salience 5 when $a : E0() from entry-point "S1" $b : E2($t : tag, this before[0ms,50ms] $a) then insertLogical(new D($t)); end
rule RN salience 9 when not D() P() then end
rule RL salience 1 when P() then end
_finding: D-176 OPEN LEDGER, finding CORRECTED AT D-177 (the original asserted 'Drools reopens the pick pre-fire' — FALSE; it pointed at the caveated D-106 executor for no reason; why the old text was wrong: the pinned output was UNDERDETERMINED — [TJ1,RN,TJ1,RL] is consistent with both pre-pick landing and reach-landing-plus-reopen, and one reading was written down as fact without a cell that could tell them apart (see the D-177 METHOD LAW: a pin is an interpretation of a probe; an underdetermined output is not a finding).) THE MECHANISM (D-177 LANDING LAW, interposer-discriminated hm1/hm1b): in a STREAM session an explicit delete's teardown lands at the delete's PROPAGATION, pre-pick — the delete kills the UNMARKED partner — the law keys on death cause, not the mark carrier; the executor never reopens a pick and the cf5x17 static return is CORRECT. Oracle 3x: [TJ1, RN, TJ1, RL]. Engine on HEAD defers the k>=2 teardown to the victim's pop eval and under-fires RN ([TJ1,TJ1,RL]). FIX validated-then-REVERTED at D-177 (tms_eager_break k=1 scope lifts for stream explicit deletes; diff verbatim in D-177): this pin PASSES under it. Graduates at the landing.
